---
title: "Write-Up"
format: html
---


```{python}
import geopandas as gpd
import pandas as pd
import os
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from shapely import wkt
import contextily as cx
import csv
import numpy as np
from geopy.geocoders import ArcGIS
import time
from shapely.geometry import Point
import requests
from datetime import datetime
```

```{python}
DATA = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)), "Final_Data")
demo = pd.read_csv(os.path.join(DATA, "cook_county_high_school_demographics.csv"))
transport = pd.read_csv(os.path.join(DATA,'CTA_-_Bus_Routes_20260129.csv'))
academic = pd.read_csv(os.path.join(DATA,"panel_yx_highschools_base_new.csv"))
cook = gpd.read_file(os.path.join(DATA,'cook_county/PVS_25_v2_tracts2020_17031.shp'))
income = pd.read_csv(os.path.join(DATA,'cook_tract_income_v7_2016_2024.csv'))
desert = pd.read_csv(os.path.join(DATA,'cook_food_access_2019.csv'))
seg = pd.read_csv(os.path.join(DATA,'school_segregation_all_final.csv'))

school_loc = gpd.read_file(os.path.join(DATA,'Public_School_Locations_2021-22.geojson'))
df = pd.read_csv(os.path.join(DATA,'final_merged.csv'))
```

```{python}
cook_3857 = cook.to_crs(epsg=3857)
cook_3857.head()
threshold = 0.5
cook_clean = cook_3857.loc[:, cook_3857.apply(lambda col: ((col.isna()) | (col.isnull())).mean() <= threshold)]
cook_clean = cook_clean[['TRACTID', 'TRACTLABEL', 'POP20', 'geometry']]
cook_clean['TRACTID'] = cook_clean['TRACTID'].astype(int)

income['year'].unique()
income['median_hh_income'] = income['median_hh_income'].apply(
    lambda x: np.nan if x is not None and x < 0 else x
)
income['GEOID'] = income['GEOID'].astype(int)
cook_income = pd.merge(cook_clean, income, left_on='TRACTID', right_on='GEOID', how='right')
cook_income = cook_income[cook_income['TRACTID'].notna()]
```

```{python}
school_loc = school_loc[school_loc['STATE']=='IL']
school_loc
```
```{python}

demo = demo.replace("*", np.nan)
demographic = demo.loc[:, demo.isna().mean() <= 0.5]
demographic = demographic[demographic['County']=='Cook']
demographic = demographic[['RCDTS', 'School Name']]

academic.columns
academic_seg = pd.merge(academic, seg, left_on=['school_name', 'year'], right_on=['school', 'year'], how='left')

demo_aca = pd.merge(demographic, academic_seg, left_on = 'RCDTS', right_on ='school_id', how='outer')
school_loc_unique = school_loc.drop_duplicates(subset='NAME')

merge_schools = pd.merge(demo_aca, school_loc_unique, left_on='School Name', right_on='NAME', how='left')
school_loc_unique.head()
###########################################################################

missing_schools = [
    {'School Name': 'Acero Chtr Sch Network -  Major Hector P Garcia MD H S', 'LAT': 41.8085443, 'LON': -87.7333591},
    {'School Name': 'Acero Chtr Sch Network- Sor Juana Ines de la Cruz K-12', 'LAT': 42.0160989, 'LON': -87.6871158},
    {'School Name': 'Collins Academy STEAM High School', 'LAT': 41.8640799, 'LON': -87.7036933}
]

for school in missing_schools:
    mask = merge_schools["School Name"] == school["School Name"]
    merge_schools.loc[mask, "LAT"] = school["LAT"]
    merge_schools.loc[mask, "LON"] = school["LON"]

###########################################################################

merge_schools_geo = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    merge_schools,
    geometry=[Point(xy) for xy in zip(merge_schools['LON'], merge_schools['LAT'])],
    crs="EPSG:4326"  # WGS84 lat/lon
)

merge_schools_geo = merge_schools_geo.to_crs(epsg=3857)
merge_schools_geo = merge_schools_geo.to_crs(cook_clean.crs)



```

```{python}
transport['geometry'] = transport['the_geom'].apply(wkt.loads)

gdf_transport = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    transport,
    geometry="geometry",
    crs="EPSG:4326"
)
gdf_transport_3857 = gdf_transport.to_crs(epsg=3857)
```

```{python}
merge_schools_geo.crs
cook_income.crs
```
```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 10))
merge_schools_geo.plot(
    ax=ax,
    color="red",
    markersize=20,
    alpha=0.7,
    label="schools",
)
ax.set(title='test')

```

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 10))

cook_income.plot(
    column='median_hh_income',
    scheme='Quantiles',
    k=5,
    cmap='Blues',                    
    ax=ax,
    edgecolor='black',                 
    linewidth=0.5
)
merge_schools_geo.plot(
    ax=ax,
    color="red",
    markersize=20,
    alpha=0.7,
    label="schools"
)
gdf_transport_3857.plot(
    ax=ax,
    linewidth=1,
    edgecolor="green",
    alpha=0.5,
    label="Transport"
)
ax.set(title='Median Income')
ax.legend(
          fontsize=10,
          frameon=False,
          title="Map Layers")
cx.add_basemap(
    ax,
    source=cx.providers.CartoDB.Positron
)

ax.set_axis_off()
plt.show()

```

```{python}

import numpy as np
import plotly.graph_objects as go
df["income_quintile"] = pd.qcut(
    df['median_hh_income'].rank(method="first"),   # rank first to handle ties
    5,
    labels=["Q1 (Lowest)", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4", "Q5 (Highest)"],
)
QUINTILE_COLORS = {
    "Q1 (Lowest)":  "#d73027",   # red
    "Q2":           "#fc8d59",   # orange
    "Q3":           "#fee08b",   # yellow
    "Q4":           "#91bfdb",   # light blue
    "Q5 (Highest)": "#4575b4",   # dark blue
}
def plot_scatter_with_ols(
    df,
    x_col,
    y_col,
    x_label=None,
    y_label=None,
    title=None,
    quintile_col="income_quintile",
    y_max=100,
):
    """
    Scatter plot of two columns with OLS trendline, colored by quintiles.
    
    Parameters:
    -----------
    df : pandas.DataFrame
        The data.
    x_col : str
        Column name for x-axis.
    y_col : str
        Column name for y-axis.
    x_label : str, optional
        Label for x-axis. Defaults to x_col.
    y_label : str, optional
        Label for y-axis. Defaults to y_col.
    title : str, optional
        Plot title. Defaults to "{x_col} vs {y_col}".
    quintile_col : str
        Column name containing quintile categories. Default is 'income_quintile'.
    y_max : float
        Maximum value for y-axis (e.g., 100). Default is 100.
    """
    
    sub = df[[x_col, y_col, quintile_col]].dropna(subset=[x_col, y_col])
    if sub.empty:
        print(f"No data for {x_col} vs {y_col}")
        return
    
    x_arr = sub[x_col].values.astype(float)
    y_arr = sub[y_col].values.astype(float)
    
    # OLS trendline
    slope, intercept_ols = np.polyfit(x_arr, y_arr, 1)
    x_line = np.linspace(x_arr.min(), x_arr.max(), 300)
    y_line = intercept_ols + slope * x_line
    in_bounds = (y_line >= 0) & (y_line <= y_max)
    x_line = x_line[in_bounds]
    y_line = y_line[in_bounds]
    
    # Plotly figure
    fig = go.Figure()
    
    # Scatter by quintile
    for q_label, color in QUINTILE_COLORS.items():
        mask = sub[quintile_col] == q_label
        if mask.any():
            fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
                x=sub.loc[mask, x_col],
                y=sub.loc[mask, y_col],
                mode="markers",
                marker=dict(size=5, opacity=0.45, color=color),
                name=q_label
            ))
    
    # OLS trendline
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=x_line,
        y=y_line,
        mode="lines",
        line=dict(color="#FF4B4B", width=3),
        name=f"OLS (slope={slope:+.3f})"
    ))
    
    fig.update_layout(
        title=title if title is not None else f"{x_col} vs {y_col}",
        xaxis_title=x_label if x_label is not None else x_col,
        yaxis_title=y_label if y_label is not None else y_col,
        yaxis_range=[0, y_max],
        height=400,
        width=600,
        margin=dict(l=40, r=20, t=50, b=40),
        legend_title="Income Quintile"
    )
    
    fig.show()

```

```{python}
df.columns
```
```{python}
plot_scatter_with_ols(
    df=df,
    x_col="x_ap_coursework",
    y_col="y_ela_prof",
    x_label="AP Coursework",
    y_label="Post-Test ELA Score",
    title="AP Coursework & ELA Scores"
)
```
